'''Iyred''' was a [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="Socorro" /> located in the [[Iyred system/Legends|Iyred system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Kibilini sector/Legends|Kibilini sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> At one point, its surface was covered with tropical<ref name="CSWE Iyred" /> and [[Temperature/Legends|temperate]]<ref name="Socorro" /> [[Forest/Legends|forests]].<ref name="CSWE Iyred" /> That habitat was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Bha'lir|Black Bha'lir]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of large [[Cat/Legends|feline]] [[Diet/Legends|predators]].<ref name="Socorro" />

Eventually, Iyred became the site of construction efforts<ref name="CSWE Iyred" /> that brought deforestation and desolation to the world. As the Black Bha'lir's [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]] sources became depleted, the creatures faced starvation and ultimately were officially listed as extinct. However, the [[Black Bha'lir]], an interstellar organization of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] that had adopted the creature as a mascot,<ref name="Socorro" /> led several missions to rescue the large felines,<ref name="CSWE Iyred" /> with small numbers of the creatures eventually being raised at isolated offworld refuges.<ref name="Socorro" />

==Behind the scenes==
Iyred was mentioned in ''[[The Black Sands of Socorro]]'', a [[1997]] sourcebook authored by [[Patricia A. Jackson]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Socorro" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Iyred [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world Iyred, in grid square Q-17.<ref name="TEA" />
